commands.repair_reason: External branch needs contributor rebase, concrete fixes, and real behavior proof; it is not a safe automated repair lane while proof is missing.; Needs attention: Needs attention because the PR touches security-sensitive logging and fallback paths and introduces concrete runtime regressions, though it adds no dependency or workflow supply-chain surface.; Review finding: [P1] Pass a valid runtime to cleanup logging... (sha=ff921b0ad71cd52ee6a033c9284891b32ce16c54)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds logging around TLS chmod failures, fs-safe handle-close cleanup, and malformed pairing allow-from JSON, and rewrites internal hook registration to avoid a non-null assertion.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The PR passes { error } to logWarn where current main expects a RuntimeEnv, and the malformed allow-from path logs before the fallback cache update; I did not run a live failure scenario because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: External branch needs contributor rebase, concrete fixes, and real behavior proof; it is not a safe automated repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: fix: improve error handling and logging for security-critical operations
target.body:
- infra/tls/gateway.ts: Log TLS permission restriction failures instead of silent failure
- infra/fs-safe.ts: Add safeCloseHandle() helper with logging for file handle cleanup
- pairing/pairing-store.ts: Log malformed JSON errors in config files
- hooks/internal-hooks.ts: Remove unnecessary non-null assertion

Addresses security audit findings from comprehensive codebase review. All changes maintain backward compatibility - only adds logging, no behavior changes.
